source_jobs.title: Commercial FP&A Manager
source_jobs.description:
If you’ve been looking to join a fast-growing startup with a bold vision of a world where doing good is built into everything we do, then you’ve found the right place.

Backed by General Catalyst, the same investors behind Stripe and Airbnb, we’re building something truly extraordinary.




OUR MISSION

Our mission at Goodstack is to revolutionize how the world does good 🌎.

As a fast growth, AI-native social impact startup, we power global change through technology. We enable companies to seamlessly integrate positive impact into what they do through a unified platform while supporting nonprofits in gaining access to cutting-edge technology and finding new funding streams.

Global brands, including Google, OpenAI, TikTok, LinkedIn, HSBC, Atlassian and Twilio - as well as thousands of nonprofits, including the Red Cross, Cancer Research, and Oxfam - use Goodstack to make a difference.

In 2025 alone, we facilitated $5 billion in donations to good causes. But this is just the beginning. We're building the world’s leading platform that powers donations to nonprofits globally.

To keep our momentum and deliver consistently high value to our partners, we need incredible people to help us on our journey – we need you 🫵




JOIN US AS AN FP&A AND COMMERCIAL FINANCE MANAGER!

Working with the VP Finance, you will be the go-to commercial finance partner for the Founder-CEO, COO and leadership team, building the FP&A function from scratch and owning everything from the financial model to analysing business line performance to transaction support.

This role sits at the intersection of finance, strategy and commercial operations — it is not a back-office role. If you want to understand every part of the business, influence decisions at the highest level, and help build a finance function that is ready for a transaction, this is the job.



YOUR MISSION WILL BE TO:



FP&A & FINANCIAL PLANNING

 - Build and maintain the company's financial model, revenue, cost, headcount, and cash, with scenario and sensitivity analysis.

 - Own the annual budget and rolling forecast process, working with all business functions to set and track targets.

 - Produce monthly budget vs actuals variance analysis with clear commentary and insight for the leadership team and board.

 - Develop business line P&Ls to give the business visibility into how each product and segment is performing.

 - Build and own the investor-ready financial narrative and supporting materials for fundraising and M&A processes.


COMMERCIAL FINANCE & BUSINESS PARTNERING

 - Act as the primary commercial finance partner to the VP Finance and leadership team — turning around ad hoc analysis, modelling, and commercial sense-checks quickly and independently.

 - Support pricing decisions, unit economics analysis, COGS deep-dives and margin improvement initiatives.

 - Partner with the GTM team on pipeline-to-revenue reporting, cohort analysis and commercial deal structuring.

 - Evaluate new commercial opportunities, partnerships and market expansion scenarios with financial rigour.


REPORTING & INSIGHTS

 - Produce high-quality board and investor reporting packs — clear, well-structured, insight-driven.

 - Develop dashboards and automated reporting to give the business real-time visibility into financial performance.

 - Own the weekly financial reporting cadence for internal leadership.
   


THIS ROLE IS A PERFECT MATCH FOR YOU IF YOU HAVE:



SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

 - ACA, ACCA or CIMA qualified (or equivalent).

 - 5+ years in FP&A, commercial finance, strategic finance, or top-tier strategy consulting, ideally with a mix of scale-up and more established business experience.

 - Proven track record of building financial models from scratch, comfortable with multi-entity, multi-currency models.

 - Experience in a commercially facing role, you have worked directly with C-level operators, founders, or senior leadership, and are used to being the go-to person for financial answer

source_jobs.title: Revenue Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
If you’ve been looking to join a fast-growing startup with a bold vision of a world where doing good is built into everything we do, then you’ve found the right place.

Backed by General Catalyst, the same investors behind Stripe and Airbnb, we’re building something truly extraordinary.




OUR MISSION

Our mission at Goodstack is to revolutionize how the world does good 🌎.

As a fast growth, AI-native social impact startup, we power global change through technology. We enable companies to seamlessly integrate positive impact into what they do through a unified platform while supporting nonprofits in gaining access to cutting-edge technology and finding new funding streams.

Global brands, including Google, OpenAI, TikTok, LinkedIn, HSBC, Atlassian and Twilio - as well as thousands of nonprofits, including the Red Cross, Cancer Research, and Oxfam - use Goodstack to make a difference.

In 2025 alone, we facilitated $5 billion in donations to good causes. But this is just the beginning. We're building the world’s leading platform that powers donations to nonprofits globally.

To keep our momentum and deliver consistently high value to our partners, we need incredible people to help us on our journey – we need you 🫵




JOIN US AS A AS REVENUE OPERATIONS MANAGER TO HELP BUILD THE OPERATING ENGINE BEHIND OUR GROWTH

You will work across Sales, Finance, Marketing, Solutions, Customer Success, and Ops to make sure the revenue engine is well run, well instrumented, and built for the next stage of growth.

We are specifically looking for someone who is deeply AI-native in how they operate. This is not a traditional RevOps role focused purely on process management and reporting. We want someone who instinctively uses AI, automation and modern tooling to increase leverage, reduce manual work and accelerate decision-making across the GTM organisation. We strongly favour candidates who can demonstrate unusually high leverage through AI and automation.


AS REVENUE OPERATIONS MANAGER, YOUR MISSION WILL BE TO:


Own the GTM operating engine

 - Own HubSpot as the source of truth for GTM operations.

 - Improve workflows, data structure, automation, and reporting across the revenue org.

 - Make our systems simpler, smarter, and easier to trust.

Strengthen pipeline and reporting infrastructure

 - Build the foundations behind reliable pipeline visibility and forecast inputs.

 - Improve how stage movement, conversion, and slippage are surfaced.

 - Help leadership inspect performance with more confidence and less manual effort.

Drive process excellence

 - Own core GTM workflow design across lead-to-deal and deal-to-delivery handoffs.

 - Create clear, scalable processes that make the right behaviour easy.

 - Improve consistency across teams as we grow.

Help scale automation

 - Support the rollout of high-impact automation across the GTM motion.

 - Turn strategic direction into practical execution through tooling, workflows, and AI-enabled systems.

 - Help Goodstack operate with greater leverage as we scale.

Improve commercial workflow coordination

 - Own the operational workflow behind deal desk and other revenue-critical coordination points.

 - Help ensure deals move through the business cleanly, consistently, and with the right information in the right place.


AFTER 6 MONTHS, SUCCESS WILL LOOK LIKE:

 - Cleaner, more reliable GTM systems.

 - Stronger trust in pipeline and reporting.

 - Smoother forecasting inputs and inspection rhythms.

 - More scalable automation across key workflows.

 - Clearer ownership and less friction in revenue-critical processes.

 - A revenue engine that feels more mature, more efficient, and easier to scale.


THIS ROLE IS A PERFECT MATCH FOR YOU IF YOU ARE/HAVE:

 - A systems and process builder with strong operational instincts and enough commercial judgement to understand what matters and why.

 - Strong CRM and workflow design experience.

 - Comfortable with automation, reporting logic, and

source_jobs.title: Sales Representative
source_jobs.description:
If you’ve been looking to join a fast-growing startup with a bold vision of a world where doing good is built into everything we do, then you’ve found the right place.

Backed by General Catalyst, the same investors behind Stripe and Airbnb, we’re building something truly extraordinary.




OUR MISSION

Our mission at Goodstack is to revolutionize how the world does good 🌎.

As a fast growth, AI-native social impact startup, we power global change through technology. We enable companies to seamlessly integrate positive impact into what they do through a unified platform while supporting nonprofits in gaining access to cutting-edge technology and finding new funding streams.

Global brands, including Google, OpenAI, TikTok, LinkedIn, HSBC, Atlassian and Twilio - as well as thousands of nonprofits, including the Red Cross, Cancer Research, and Oxfam - use Goodstack to make a difference.

In 2025 alone, we facilitated $5 billion in donations to good causes. But this is just the beginning. We're building the world’s leading platform that powers donations to nonprofits globally.

To keep our momentum and deliver consistently high value to our partners, we need incredible people to help us on our journey – we need you 🫵




JOIN US AS A SALES REPRESENTATIVE TO HELP NONPROFITS DISCOVER BETTER FUNDING OPPORTUNITIES!

We’re looking for a proactive, empathetic, and commercially curious Sales Representative to help nonprofits discover Goodstack GrantPilot.

Goodstack GrantPilot is built to give nonprofits access to the kind of fundraising support many teams could never normally afford. GrantPilot is the first step in that mission: combining smart technology, targeted funding research, and a concierge-led experience to help nonprofits find and pursue more relevant funding opportunities.

This is a brilliant role for someone who loves speaking with people, solving practical problems and helping mission-driven teams access opportunities they may never have found alone.



AS A SALES REPRESENTATIVE, YOUR MISSION WILL BE TO:

 - Speak with inbound nonprofit leads and understand their goals, funding needs and readiness.

 - Run thoughtful discovery conversations across phone, email and video.

 - Help nonprofits understand how GrantPilot can support their fundraising ambitions.

 - Research and curate relevant funding opportunities based on each organisation’s profile.

 - Create a concierge-level experience from first touch through onboarding.

 - Keep HubSpot clean, accurate and useful.

 - Share customer insights with Sales and Product to improve our playbook, product and customer experience.

 - Help build the early GrantPilot sales motion as we learn, test and scale.


AFTER 3 MONTHS, SUCCESS WILL LOOK LIKE:

 - You understand GrantPilot, our nonprofit customers and the funding challenges they face.

 - You’re confidently turning inbound interest into qualified conversations.

 - You’re building a healthy pipeline of engaged nonprofit prospects.

 - You’re creating value before the sale through useful, tailored funding insights.

 - You’re supporting smooth onboarding and giving customers a strong first experience.

 - You’re helping shape a repeatable sales playbook for GrantPilot.


THIS ROLE IS A PERFECT MATCH FOR YOU IF YOU HAVE/ARE:

 - Based in or within commutable distance of Colorado Springs, Colorado, and are comfortable with regular in-person collaboration.

 - 1+ year of experience in sales, support, fundraising, partnerships or another customer-facing role.

 - Enjoy managing a busy pipeline and communicating across phone, email and video.

 - Ask great questions, listen closely and know how to spot where we can genuinely help.

 - Communicate clearly, warmly and with strong follow-through.

 - Are confident researching funding opportunities and turning information into useful recommendations.

 - Bring energy, curiosity and care to every customer conversation.

 - Are comfortable with ambiguity, pace and building things as we

source_jobs.title: Senior Compliance and Risk Manager
source_jobs.description:
If you’ve been looking to join a fast-growing startup with a bold vision of a world where doing good is built into everything we do, then you’ve found the right place.

Backed by General Catalyst, the same investors behind Stripe and Airbnb, we’re building something truly extraordinary.




OUR MISSION

Our mission at Goodstack is to revolutionize how the world does good 🌎.

As a fast growth, AI-native social impact startup, we power global change through technology. We enable companies to seamlessly integrate positive impact into what they do through a unified platform while supporting nonprofits in gaining access to cutting-edge technology and finding new funding streams.

Global brands, including Google, OpenAI, TikTok, LinkedIn, HSBC, Atlassian and Twilio - as well as thousands of nonprofits, including the Red Cross, Cancer Research, and Oxfam - use Goodstack to make a difference.

In 2025 alone, we facilitated $5 billion in donations to good causes. But this is just the beginning. We're building the world’s leading platform that powers donations to nonprofits globally.

To keep our momentum and deliver consistently high value to our partners, we need incredible people to help us on our journey – we need you 🫵




JOIN US AS A SENIOR COMPLIANCE & RISK MANAGER!

Goodstack is building the infrastructure that powers charitable giving globally. As we scale our platform, Foundation network, and payments ecosystem, we're looking for an experienced compliance leader to evolve our compliance capabilities.

This is an opportunity to shape Goodstack’s compliance programme, building practical, scalable frameworks that support our continued growth.

You'll work closely with teams across Product, Engineering, Operations, Finance, and Leadership to embed compliance into how we design, launch, and scale our products and services.

Reporting to the Head of Global Giving, you'll own Goodstack's compliance programme across both our charitable and payments activities.


AS SENIOR COMPLIANCE & RISK MANAGER, YOUR MISSION WILL BE TO:


COMPLIANCE STRATEGY & GOVERNANCE

 - Lead and evolve Goodstack’s compliance approach as the business scales.

 - Design practical, risk-based controls across payments, charitable giving, and Foundation operations.

 - Act as a trusted partner on compliance risk, regulatory change, and industry best practice in fintech and payments.

 - Ensure our platform and Foundation network operate within relevant regulatory and governance requirements.


COMPLIANCE & PAYMENTS OPERATIONS

 - Build scalable approaches to KYC, AML, sanctions screening, transaction monitoring, and enhanced due diligence.

 - Own the end-to-end compliance control framework for high-risk users/orgs, transactions, and partners.

 - Improve and automate compliance processes to support high-volume, global payment flows.

 - Lead compliance training and embed good practice across operational teams.


PRODUCT & BUSINESS PARTNERSHIP

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to design compliance into systems, workflows, and user journeys from day one.

 - Help define how controls are implemented in product (rules, triggers, thresholds, monitoring, escalation paths).

 - Act as a key decision-maker in product development, balancing risk, customer experience, and commercial priorities.

 - Support launch readiness for new products, markets, and payment flows.


MONITORING, RISK & REPORTING

 - Monitor compliance performance across live systems and transactions.

 - Investigate and resolve complex or high-risk issues in real time.

 - Translate regulatory and scheme requirements into clear, implementable product and operational requirements.

 - Provide clear input to leadership on risk trade-offs and emerging issues.


TEAM LEADERSHIP

 - Build, mentor, and lead a high-performing compliance team.

 - Establish clear processes, responsibilities, and performance standards across the function.

 - Act as an escalation point for complex compliance and risk deci

source_jobs.title: Grant Delivery Specialist
source_jobs.description:
If you’ve been looking to join a fast-growing startup with a bold vision of a world where doing good is built into everything we do, then you’ve found the right place.

Backed by General Catalyst, the same investors behind Stripe and Airbnb, we’re building something truly extraordinary.




OUR MISSION

Our mission at Goodstack is to revolutionize how the world does good 🌎.

As a fast growth, AI-native social impact startup, we power global change through technology. We enable companies to seamlessly integrate positive impact into what they do through a unified platform while supporting nonprofits in gaining access to cutting-edge technology and finding new funding streams.

Global brands, including Google, OpenAI, TikTok, LinkedIn, HSBC, Atlassian and Twilio - as well as thousands of nonprofits, including the Red Cross, Cancer Research, and Oxfam - use Goodstack to make a difference.

In 2025 alone, we facilitated $5 billion in donations to good causes. But this is just the beginning. We're building the world’s leading platform that powers donations to nonprofits globally.

To keep our momentum and deliver consistently high value to our partners, we need incredible people to help us on our journey – we need you 🫵




JOIN US AS GRANT DELIVERY SPECIALIST TO ACT AS OUR LEAD EXPERT REVIEWER!

We’re looking for an exceptionally thoughtful, detail-driven Grant Delivery Specialist to lead high-quality grant research, writing and review for our nonprofit partners.

You’ll help nonprofits access more funding, faster – identifying relevant opportunities, shaping strong applications and making sure every draft is accurate, compelling and genuinely useful.

If you have a strong grant writing background, enjoy turning complex programme information into clear funding narratives, and want your work to support organisations doing meaningful social impact work, this could be the perfect role for you.

 


AS A GRANT DELIVERY SPECIALIST, YOUR MISSION WILL BE TO:

 - Support nonprofit partners: Lead onboarding, proactive check-ins and clear communication across calls, email and key touchpoints.

 - Research funding opportunities: Identify, evaluate and curate relevant grants tailored to each organisation’s mission, programmes, eligibility and funding needs.

 - Draft excellent applications: Write compelling LOIs, grant applications and supporting narratives aligned to funder requirements.

 - Use AI to increase quality and pace: Confidently prompt, guide and review AI-assisted outputs to accelerate research, drafting and iteration without compromising accuracy, judgement or tone.

 - Act as lead expert reviewer: Review drafts so they are accurate, persuasive, funder-ready and easy for nonprofits to use.

 - Manage delivery: Balance pace, quality and care across multiple partner relationships, keeping work moving and expectations clear.

 - Build deep context: Work closely with around 10–15 nonprofit partners at any given time, understanding their goals, programmes and impact.

 - Improve how we work: Help strengthen our grant support process so we can serve more nonprofits well, without compromising quality.


AFTER 3 MONTHS, SUCCESS WILL LOOK LIKE:

 - Producing high-quality grant drafts at a consistent pace.

 - Nonprofit partners feel supported, understood, and confident in the work they receive.

 - Funding opportunities are well-matched, relevant, and clearly explained.

 - AI is used thoughtfully to improve speed, consistency, and quality.

 - Drafts are accurate, thoughtful, and tailored to each funder.

 - Risks, blockers, or deadline concerns are raised early.

 - Our grant support process becomes clearer, stronger, and easier to scale.


THIS ROLE IS A PERFECT MATCH FOR YOU IF YOU HAVE:

 - A STEM degree from a recognised university, or equivalent evidence of strong analytical thinking.

 - 5 years’ grant writing experience, ideally for nonprofits, charities or mission-led organisations.

 - Understanding of funding landscapes, eligi
